title: e2e: GetSafeIPAddress() replaces GetRandomIPAddress
body:
For tests that use '--ip XX', random IP allocation is not
working well. Switch instead to a deterministic algorithm
with CPU affinity and a fudge factor for CNI.

Fixes: #18855 

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Converted to draft. Playing with what I think is a more readable form.

I think I'm happy-ish with this approach. I skimmed through [f37 root logs](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5220390251790336/html/int-podman-fedora-37-root-host-sqlite.log.html) (CNI), searching in-page for `10.88`, and see that that CNI does indeed seem to allocate based on most-recently-used+1. So there's still a collision risk under some timings but without a way to control CNI's behavior I can find no way to prevent this.

Also, I'm much happier with the `[2]uint8` approach and am prepared to squash that for the final version.

[EDIT] obviously I've been testing this on my no-flake-retries PR, because duh.

Sigh. Still seeing [dup-ip flakes](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6706881452834816/html/int-podman-fedora-37-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-start-podman-start-container-retains-the-HOME-env-if-present--1):
```
[It] podman start container retains the HOME env if present
...
# podman [options] restart 244462f39074f7d07d229f673f4b61adbd8eedf88744bce5d9adcb037fe83697
time="2023-06-15T07:24:56-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to load cached network config: network podman not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network podman from disk"
Error: plugin type="bridge" failed (add): cni plugin bridge failed: failed to allocate for range 0: 10.88.129.35 has been allocated to 244462f39074f7d07d229f673f4b61adbd8eedf88744bce5d9adcb037fe83697, duplicate allocation is not allowed
```

> Sigh. Still seeing [dup-ip flakes](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6706881452834816/html/int-podman-fedora-37-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-start-podman-start-container-retains-the-HOME-env-if-present--1):
> 
> ```
> [It] podman start container retains the HOME env if present
> ...
> # podman [options] restart 244462f39074f7d07d229f673f4b61adbd8eedf88744bce5d9adcb037fe83697
> time="2023-06-15T07:24:56-05:00" level=warning msg="Failed to load cached network config: network podman not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network podman from disk"
> Error: plugin type="bridge" failed (add): cni plugin bridge failed: failed to allocate for range 0: 10.88.129.35 has been allocated to 244462f39074f7d07d229f673f4b61adbd8eedf88744bce5d9adcb037fe83697, duplicate allocation is not allowed
> ```

This is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18753, I actually do not think they are the same issue.
This test never requests a static ip so it should not run into this problem. The bug here is that network cleanup clearly did not work and thus CNI still sees the old ip for this container id thus rejecting to hand out a new ip for it.

Note that the errors explicitly says `duplicate allocation is not allowed` not the `requested IP address X is not available`, you are only fixing the later. The former must be a real podman bug.

>This is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18753, I actually do not think they are the same issue.

I think you're right. Thank you for catching that. I've squashed, repushed, and removed the Draft marking.

/lgtm
Pushing this through to pickup the fix, Paul left a possible minor follow-up comment to consider @edsantiago
